title: pyrrocoma eriopoda
intro: Pyrrocoma eriopoda, also known as Rocky Mountain goldenrod, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. It is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States, where it grows in dry, rocky areas and is often found in association with sagebrush and other shrubs. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Pyrrocoma eriopoda include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments, as well as to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
